state_object: gift_card.redeemed_by flag and wallets.balance row for the redeeming user

hypotheses.expected_serial_outcome: First call credits the wallet once and sets redeemed_by=user_id; second call returns 409/410 (already redeemed).
hypotheses.expected_race_outcome: Both reads observe redeemed_by IS NULL before either write; both UPDATEs commit and balance is credited twice.
hypotheses.indicator: Wallet balance increases by 2 * code.value after a 20-request burst, or two 200 OK responses both containing the same {"credited": <value>} payload.

hypotheses.expected_serial_outcome: Whichever request lands first claims the code; the other receives 409.
hypotheses.expected_race_outcome: Both accounts pass the redeemed_by IS NULL check; both wallets get credited from a single-use code.
hypotheses.indicator: Both /api/v1/gift-cards/redeem calls return 200 with non-null credited value, AND a follow-up GET on the code shows redeemed_by set to only one of the users.

hypotheses.expected_serial_outcome: Second withdraw fails with insufficient_funds once the first commits.
hypotheses.expected_race_outcome: Both SELECTs see balance >= 100; both UPDATEs subtract 100, leaving balance negative.
hypotheses.indicator: Two 200 OK transfer confirmations and a subsequent GET /api/v1/wallet returning balance < 0, or both account ledgers showing successful deposits totalling more than the original wallet balance.

hypotheses.expected_serial_outcome: Second request returns the cached response of the first without re-crediting.
hypotheses.expected_race_outcome: Because the key is logged but not enforced, both requests are processed end-to-end like the double_redeem case.
hypotheses.indicator: Both responses carry a different transaction_id even though Idempotency-Key was identical.

non_targets: GET /api/v1/wallet — read-only and not state-changing on its own; ruled out as a primitive (still useful as the indicator oracle).
summary: wallet_double_spend is the spiciest: read-modify-write on balance with no row lock means a single-packet burst can split one balance across multiple bank accounts.
